title: Welcome to my website!
text:
What you’ll find while you’re here:
I’m currently a graduate student at Boston University, pursuing my master’s degree in Applied Business Analytics. As you make your way through my website, you’ll learn more about my educational and professional background.





Out in the Wild
When I’m not on campus or in the office, here’s where you might find me:
Traveling, running, reading, or trying a new coffee spot!

title: Contact Me
text:
Please use one of the following links to connect with me:
Feel free to send me an email, add me on LinkedIn, or download my up-to-date resume.
Thank you for stopping by my Quarto website!

title: Projects
text: My academic journey has encouraged my to explore and benefit from a variety of team projects. Through collaboration, I’ve been able to strengthen my communication, leadership, project management, and technical skills. I’ve found the following projects to be particularly educational and contructive forms of hands-on coursework application.

title: Projects
section: Data Science & Business Analytics Job Market Trends in 2024
text:
Data Science & Business Analytics Job Market Trends in 2024
Data-driven analysis of business analytics, data science, and machine learning trends found in 2024 job postings
Upon my intriduction to big data and cloud analytics for business, I explored job market trends amongst industries alongside a group of my classmates. I learned and applied a variety of new technical skills as I navigated AWS academy, Visual Studio Code, and GitHub for the first time.

title: Projects
section: Microbrewery Business Plan in South End, Boston
text:
Microbrewery Business Plan in South End, Boston
The development of a managerial report to propose a microbrewery business plan for an existing restaurant in the Boston area
For a quantitative and qualitative decision making class, we formed groups to apply our business structure coursework to a real-life scenario. It was my first project in a new field, and the adjustment from engineering to business coursework was a welcomed challenge.

title: Projects
text:
Engineering Senior Design Project
Decarbonization of the UCONN Campus by 2030
During the senior year of my undergraduate degree, I formalized a plan to help the campus reach carbon neutrality with two of my classmates. At the time, UConn was emiting approximately 108,000 metric tons of carbon dioxide per year.
Our design project was chosen because of our shared interest in sustainability and how clean energy could be introduced in a cost effective way. Our solution combined the use of renewable energy sources and conservation policies. We created an energy balance and a mass balance to outline a net-zero carbon emmision future.

title: About Me
text:
Master of Science in Applied Business Analytics - In Progress
My goal is to use the combination of my degrees as a bridge between business development and the engineering mindset to pursue my interest in process improvement and optimization.

title: About Me
text:
University of Connecticut
Bachelor of Science in Engineering
During my time at UConn I developed a strong foundation in fluid mechanics, process kinetics, thermodynamics, and air pollution control. I was able to simultaneously grow my passion for sustainable solutions.

title: About Me
text:
Straumann Group
Quality Assurance Biotech Co-op
I was lucky enough to find multiple mentors and helping hands during my time in quality assurance. I enjoyed my ability to collaborate with various departments and explore the different fields that my technical expertise could be applied to.

title: About Me
section: The Hole in the Wall Gang Camp
text:
The Hole in the Wall Gang Camp
Volunteering and Fundraising
Since my junior year of college, I began to spend a lot of my spare time with the Hole in the Wall Gang Camp. It’s a summer camp that provides “a different kind of healing” to children with serious illnesses and their families. I have the pleasure of meeting the kindest people from all different paths of life. Though I’m proud of all my accomplishments, the time I spend at camp is by far my proudest.
